facts.text: East Patchogue is a hamlet, meaning it has no official boundaries or government and is simply a named, settled area.
Patchogue once served as Suffolk County’s major shopping and commerce hub, a legacy that still echoes in its bustling streets.
The Smith‑Rourke House and Swan River Schoolhouse in East Patchogue are listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
The local history room’s eclectic collection emphasizes Long Island and Patchogue‑Medford history, featuring books, maps, postcards, and oral histories.
A dedicated local history website offers over 100 organized web pages with chronologies, digital materials, and handy links for researchers.
East Patchogue includes the eastern part of Patchogue in the wider sense and stretches eastward toward the village of Bellport.
